assumptions: The question is about consolidated financial statements, not separate or individual financial statements.
The intragroup dividend has already been recognised as a receivable/payable, so the exposure considered is a recognised intragroup balance rather than a forecast transaction.
The risk at issue is foreign currency risk, and no facts are provided suggesting the investment-entity exception.

approaches.label: Ordinary intragroup item not eligible in consolidation
approaches.reasoning: IFRS 9 generally allows hedged items in consolidation only for exposures with external parties. Intragroup items are excluded unless a specific exception applies.
approaches.practical_implication: Do not designate the dividend receivable in a consolidated hedge relationship unless it fits a stated IFRS 9 exception.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.5 excludes intragroup items from hedged items in consolidated financial statements, apart from stated exceptions.

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of intragroup monetary item
approaches.reasoning: IFRS 9 permits the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to be a hedged item in consolidation when the related FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
conditions: The dividend receivable/payable is a monetary intragroup item
The entities involved have different functional currencies so FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation
The hedge is formally designated and documented at inception and meets hedge effectiveness requirements
approaches.practical_implication: The FX risk on the recognised intragroup dividend balance can be documented only if it creates a residual consolidated FX exposure and all hedge-accounting criteria are met.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.6 allows foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements.
Paragraph 6.4.1 requires formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.reasoning: The intragroup forecast-transaction exception applies only to highly probable forecast intragroup transactions whose FX risk affects consolidated profit or loss. A booked dividend receivable is no longer a forecast transaction.
approaches.practical_implication: This route does not support hedge designation once the dividend has already been recognised as a receivable.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraphs 6.3.6, B6.3.5 and B6.3.6 permit certain highly probable forecast intragroup transactions to be hedged in consolidation.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.reasoning: A net investment hedge is a separate hedge model for foreign operations' net assets. It does not apply to an intragroup dividend receivable by analogy, but may be relevant if the intended hedged item is the net investment itself.
conditions: The exposure hedged is the net investment in a foreign operation included in consolidated financial statements
The hedged risk is the FX difference between the foreign operation's functional currency and a parent entity's functional currency
The relationship satisfies IFRS 9 designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements
approaches.practical_implication: Possible only if the hedge is documented as a net investment hedge; it is not a basis to hedge the dividend receivable itself by analogy.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraphs 10–15 describe when foreign currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation can qualify for hedge accounting.
Paragraph 8 says the Interpretation applies only to net investment hedges and should not be applied by analogy to other hedge types.
Paragraph 6.4.1 requires formal designation, documentation and effectiveness.
